description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, giving the block a visually striking, textured appearance. The stone appears to be a dark, granite-like material with a speckled gray and black coloration, suggesting durability and resistance to weathering. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with no visible joints or separations, indicating it is a single, monolithic piece. The block is thick and robust, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment such as a sidewalk, plaza, or park. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant wear, and the overall finish is rough and matte.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, made of speckled dark granite and featuring a rough, matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the block's flat face and the horizontal grooves running across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the parallel grooves, which appear more pronounced from this angle.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the flat face and horizontal grooves on the top surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep, evenly spaced grooves across the top.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
